data.title: Is It Time To Reassess eToro Group (ETOR) After This Year’s Share Price Slide?
data.description:
• If you are wondering whether eToro Group at around US$29.05 is a bargain or a value trap, this breakdown is designed to help you frame that question clearly.
  • The share price closed at US$29.05, with a 1.3% decline over the past week, a 10.5% decline over the past month, and an 18.6% decline year to date, which may signal shifting views on both risk and potential.
  • Recent coverage has focused on eToro Group as a publicly listed trading platform provider that gives investors access to a wide range of assets, something that often attracts attention when markets are active. There has also been broader discussion about listed trading platforms and how their business models are being reassessed as conditions change.
  • Simply Wall St assigns eToro Group a valuation score of 5/6. The sections that follow will unpack what that means by walking through different valuation methods before finishing with a way to pull all those signals together into a clearer picture of value.

eToro Group delivered 0.0% returns over the last year. See how this stacks up to the rest of the Capital Markets industry.

Advertisement

Approach 1: eToro Group Excess Returns Analysis

The Excess Returns model looks at how much profit a company is expected to generate over and above the return that shareholders require, then converts those extra earnings into a per share value.

For eToro Group, the starting point is the equity base. Book Value is $16.83 per share and Stable Book Value is $15.90 per share, both derived from the median level over the past 5 years. On that base, Stable EPS is $3.35 per share, sourced from the median Return on Equity over the same period, with an Average Return on Equity of 21.04%.

The model compares this to a Cost of Equity of $1.52 per share, which implies an Excess Return of $1.82 per share. In other words, the analysis assumes eToro Group is earning more than the required return on its equity, and that this pattern continues at a stable level.

Using these inputs, the Excess Returns Model arrives at an estimated intrinsic value of about $45.53 per share, compared with the recent share price of $29.05. That gap reflects an implied 36.2% discount, which indicates the stock is trading below this model’s estimate of value.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Our Excess Returns analysis suggests eToro Group is undervalued by 36.2%. Track this in your watchlist or portfolio, or discover 61 more high quality undervalued stocks.

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for eToro Group.

Approach 2: eToro Group Price vs Earnings

For profitable companies, the P/E ratio is a useful shorthand because it links what you pay for the stock directly to the earnings the business is currently generating. It helps you see how many dollars of price the market is assigning to each dollar of earnings.

What counts as a “normal” or “fair” P/E depends on how the market views growth potential and risk. Higher expected growth or lower perceived risk can support a higher P/E, while slower growth or higher risk usually points to a lower multiple.

eToro Group currently trades on a P/E of 11.16x. That sits below the Capital Markets industry average of 30.75x and also below the peer average of 12.91x. Simply Wall St’s Fair Ratio for eToro Group is 16.98x. This is its proprietary estimate of what the P/E might be given factors such as earnings growth, industry, profit margin, market cap and risk profile.

The Fair Ratio can be more informative than a simple comparison to peers or the broad industry because it is tailored to the company’s own fundamentals rather than assuming that all businesses in the group deserve similar multiples. On this basis, eToro Group’s P/E of 11.16x sits below the Fair Ratio of 16.98x. This points to the shares trading at a lower multiple than this framework suggests.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Upgrade Your Decision Making: Choose your eToro Group Narrative

Earlier it was mentioned that there is an even better way to understand valuation, so Narratives are introduced as a simple way for you to connect your view of eToro Group to the numbers by writing a short story about the business, linking that story to your own revenue, earnings and margin estimates, and turning those into a Fair Value that can be compared with the current share price.

On Simply Wall St, Narratives sit on the Community page and let you see how different investors connect the same facts to very different outcomes. For example, one detailed eToro Group Narrative on NasdaqGS:ETOR sets a Fair Value of US$148.85 per share while another might be far lower, so you can quickly see how different assumptions about risk and growth translate into very different valuations.

Because Narratives are refreshed when new information such as news or earnings is added, your Fair Value view is never static. You can keep using the gap between Fair Value and Price as a clear, evolving reference point when deciding whether the stock still fits your own thesis or whether it is time to review your position.

data.description:
• Rocket Lab has completed its first dedicated launch for the European Space Agency.
  • The mission used the Electron rocket and adds ESA to Rocket Lab's roster of government clients, alongside NASA, JAXA and KASA.
  • The launch highlights growing global demand for small satellite access to space from both government and commercial customers.

For investors watching Rocket Lab (NasdaqCM:RKLB), this ESA mission comes as the stock trades at $60.93 and has shown a very large 1 year return and an increase of more than 7x over 3 years. Those gains sit alongside a 19.8% decline year to date, a 13.2% decline over the past month and a 9.4% decline over the past week, which underscores how volatile sentiment around launch providers can be.

This dedicated ESA contract adds another government partner to Rocket Lab's mix, which already includes multiple international agencies. For you, the key question is whether this broader customer base and expanding government presence align with your risk tolerance, time horizon and views on the demand for small launch and related space services.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for Rocket Lab by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on Rocket Lab.

📰 Beyond the headline: 3 risks and 1 thing going right for Rocket Lab that every investor should see.

This ESA launch is less about a single mission and more about where Rocket Lab now sits in the pecking order of trusted government launch partners. Adding ESA to existing work with NASA, JAXA and KASA broadens the pool of agency customers that can award repeat missions, which matters for both launch cadence and contract visibility. The Celeste mission also places Rocket Lab inside Europe’s satellite navigation ecosystem, an area with potential follow on work around positioning, timing, and secure communications. For you, the question is how this wider government reach fits alongside recent defense wins, such as the hypersonic HASTE contract, and long term plans around Neutron and space systems. The company is increasingly tied to national security, navigation, and Earth observation programs where reliability and schedule performance tend to matter as much as price. That can be a positive for recurring business, but it also concentrates exposure to government budgets and program decisions.

Advertisement

How This Fits Into The Rocket Lab Narrative

  • The ESA partnership supports the narrative that Rocket Lab is building an end to end space services business with strong demand from national security and civil agencies, which underpins expectations for multi year revenue growth and backlog expansion.
  • Growing dependence on government and agency contracts could challenge the narrative if large programs are delayed, reprioritized, or become more competitive as players like SpaceX, Blue Origin, and Arianespace chase similar work.
  • The Celeste mission’s focus on satellite navigation and potential downstream uses in autonomous vehicles and critical infrastructure is not fully reflected in many high level narratives that focus more on launch and Neutron than on application specific constellations.

Knowing what a company is worth starts with understanding its story. Check out one of the top narratives in the Simply Wall St Community for Rocket Lab to help decide what it's worth to you.

The Risks and Rewards Investors Should Consider

  • Higher exposure to government and agency spending means Rocket Lab is sensitive to shifts in defense and civil space budgets, procurement cycles, and political priorities across the US, Europe, and Asia.
  • Expanding relationships across ESA, NASA, JAXA and defense customers adds execution complexity, and any launch failure or schedule slip could affect its 100% mission success record for national programs and weigh on future awards.
  • A growing roster of agency partners can support a fuller launch manifest and strengthen the case that Rocket Lab is a go to option for small satellite missions, alongside larger players such as SpaceX and United Launch Alliance.
  • Success in navigation focused missions like Celeste gives Rocket Lab a reference point in areas tied to autonomous vehicles, maritime, and critical infrastructure, which may support follow on work in higher value, application specific constellations.

What To Watch Going Forward

From here, keep an eye on whether ESA returns with additional dedicated Electron missions or broadens work into other programs, and how that compares with new contracts from NASA, JAXA, defense agencies, and commercial constellation operators. The mix between government, defense, and commercial launches will shape how resilient Rocket Lab’s backlog is if any single customer group slows. It is also worth watching whether the company keeps its mission success record intact as launch cadence increases and Neutron development progresses in parallel, since reliability is central to winning repeat high value contracts.

A Discounted Cash Flow, or DCF, model estimates what a company might be worth today by projecting its future cash flows and then discounting them back to a single present value figure.

data.title: SoftBank Deepens AI Bet With US$40b Loan And OpenAI Follow On

The planned follow on stake in OpenAI connects directly with SoftBank's majority position in Arm Holdings, where OpenAI may act as an early customer for new Arm designed AI chips. That creates a close relationship between AI software and the underlying chip designs inside SoftBank's broader portfolio.

This concentration in high profile AI projects also affects SoftBank's risk profile. data.description:
Fresh off its FY 2025 update, I.D.I. Insurance (TASE:IDIN) reported Q3 revenue of ₪1.0b and basic EPS of ₪5.6, set against trailing twelve month revenue of ₪3.7b and EPS of ₪21.21 that reflect 23.6% earnings growth over the past year. Over recent periods, the company has seen revenue move from ₪3.2b to ₪3.7b on a trailing basis, with EPS stepping up from ₪14.70 to ₪21.21, while net profit margin shifted from 7.2% to 8.4%. For investors, that combination of higher earnings and firmer margins frames this release as a profitability focused story rather than just a top line update.

8.4% Net Margin Puts Profitability In Focus

  • The trailing twelve month net profit margin of 8.4%, up from 7.2% a year earlier, sits alongside Q3 2025 net income of ₪82.7 million on revenue of ₪1,038.5 million.
  • What is interesting for a more bullish view is that 23.6% earnings growth over the last year and a 5 year earnings growth rate of 8.3% sit alongside this higher margin, which suggests to bullish investors that profit efficiency is improving as the business scales. Skeptics would point out that net income in individual quarters, such as ₪82.7 million in Q3 2025 versus ₪88.5 million in Q3 2024, can move around even when the longer term margin trend looks stronger.
    • Bulls often focus on the combination of the 23.6% earnings growth and the 8.4% margin, arguing that this pairing supports the idea of a business that is not just growing, but doing so with tighter profitability.
    • More cautious investors may highlight that quarterly net income across the last six reported quarters ranges between roughly ₪59.5 million and ₪90.4 million. They note that even within an improving margin picture, individual periods can still show earnings swings that they regard as a reminder that results can be lumpy.

Curious how these profit trends fit into the wider story that other investors are building around the stock? 📊 Read the what the Community is saying about I.D.I. Insurance.

23.6% Earnings Growth Meets Modest P/E

  • Over the trailing twelve months, earnings grew 23.6% while the stock trades on a P/E of 10.1x, below the Asian insurance industry average of 11.4x and a peer average of 16.9x.
  • For a bullish read, the combination of faster earnings growth and a P/E that sits under both industry and peer averages is often used to argue that the market is not fully crediting the recent improvement. Yet the presence of only an 8.4% net margin keeps the discussion grounded, because it shows the company is still an insurer with relatively standard profitability rather than an outlier on returns.
    • Supporters of the bullish view might point out that a 10.1x P/E on a business that has grown earnings 23.6% in the last year and 8.3% per year over five years looks undemanding compared with peers at 16.9x, especially when margin has shifted from 7.2% to 8.4% over the same 12 month period.
    • Those pushing back on that bullish angle may respond that, even with these figures, insurers are often priced with a margin of safety, so a lower P/E alone, without outsize margins or other differentiators, does not automatically translate into a clear valuation gap.

Dividend Yield 6.17% But Cash Coverage Weak

  • The trailing dividend yield sits at 6.17%, while the dividend is flagged as not well covered by free cash flow, even as trailing twelve month net income totals ₪312.4 million.
  • Bears often lean on this cash coverage point, arguing that a high yield without strong free cash flow backing can be a weak link in the story. The data here supports that concern because the company combines a relatively full dividend payout with only moderate margins, despite solid earnings growth, so income focused investors may want to weigh the 6.17% headline yield against the explicit note that free cash flow coverage is weak.
    • Critics highlight that while net income over the last year reaches ₪312.4 million and the net margin is 8.4%, the lack of robust free cash flow coverage for the dividend means that management is relying on more than just cash earnings to support a 6.17% yield.
    • Supporters of the bearish stance also point to the fact that the stock trades only about 0.8% below the DCF fair value of ₪214.54 at a current share price of ₪212.80, which they see as leaving limited room for compensation if dividend coverage were to tighten further.

data.title: Novo Nordisk Voucher Win Meets Rising Scrutiny Of FDA Review Program
data.description:
• Novo Nordisk (NYSE:NVO) has received an FDA National Priority Voucher tied to a higher dose of its obesity drug Wegovy.
  • This is the fourth such voucher granted for the higher dose version, marking another key regulatory step for the product.
  • The decision comes as the FDA prepares a June public meeting to review and discuss the controversial priority voucher program.
  • Lawmakers have called for more transparency around how these vouchers are awarded and used, which could influence future regulatory processes.

Novo Nordisk enters this phase of the regulatory discussion with a current share price of $36.04. The stock has seen a 31.2% decline year to date and a 47.0% decline over the past year, while the 5 year return is 12.1%. For investors, this mix of long term gains and recent weakness provides context when assessing how new regulatory developments might affect sentiment around NYSE:NVO.

The combination of an additional priority voucher and heightened scrutiny of the program could influence how quickly and under what conditions future treatments reach the market. Investors may want to monitor the June FDA meeting and related Congressional commentary, as any policy shifts could affect both the value of existing vouchers and the regulatory path for upcoming products.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for Novo Nordisk by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on Novo Nordisk.

The new National Priority Voucher for higher dose Wegovy gives Novo Nordisk another tool to secure faster FDA review timelines, which can matter for label updates, follow on formulations, or future cardio metabolic indications. However, the value of this voucher is less straightforward with the FDA preparing a public meeting on the program and lawmakers calling for more transparency. If the program is tightened, future vouchers could carry stricter conditions, face reduced resale appeal, or be harder to obtain. For you as an investor, this turns the voucher from a simple win into a moving regulatory variable that could affect how quickly Novo Nordisk can progress obesity related treatments through the FDA and how competitors such as Eli Lilly or Pfizer may be treated under the same framework.

  • ⚠️ Changes to the priority voucher program could reduce the speed or flexibility Novo Nordisk gains from this approval, affecting timelines for future Wegovy related filings.
  • ⚠️ Greater Congressional and FDA scrutiny may lead to tighter rules, extra disclosure, or limits on how vouchers are used or transferred, which could reduce their economic usefulness.
  • 🎁 Holding another voucher gives Novo Nordisk optionality to seek accelerated review for a future submission, which can be valuable in a competitive obesity and diabetes market that includes Eli Lilly and Pfizer.
  • 🎁 Participation in the program may help Novo Nordisk stay closely aligned with evolving FDA expectations around obesity therapies and priority review criteria.

What To Watch Going Forward

From here, it is worth watching the June FDA meeting for any changes to how vouchers are awarded, priced, or applied to future drug applications, as well as any follow up guidance from lawmakers on program transparency. Also keep an eye on how Novo Nordisk chooses to use this specific voucher, whether on Wegovy related work or another asset, and how that timing compares with rival launches from Eli Lilly or Pfizer.
